1.title: Why 'Numbers'?
1.text: The book's English title comes from this census. In Hebrew it is called 'Bamidbar' ('in the wilderness'), but the Greek and Latin translations named it 'Numbers' because of the counting of the tribes.

1.title: A vast army
1.text: The total counted was 603,550 men able to go to war, and that was without the Levites, women, or children, hinting at just how large Israel's camp in the wilderness was.

2.text: Every tribe camped under its own standard and ensign, always facing the tabernacle of the congregation at the center, showing that the worship of God was the heart of Israel's camp.

3.title: Aaron's four sons
3.text: Aaron had four priestly sons: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar. Because Nadab and Abihu died childless, Israel's entire priestly line descended through Eleazar and Ithamar alone.

6.text: The word "Nazarite" comes from the Hebrew nazir, meaning "separated" or "consecrated." It was a vow any Israelite, man or woman, could take to dedicate himself wholly to the Lord for a period of time.

7.text: Everything begins the day Moses had fully set up the tabernacle and anointed and sanctified it with all its instruments and the altar — only then could the princes' offerings begin.

7.text: The chapter closes with Moses entering the tabernacle of the congregation and hearing the Lord's voice speak from off the mercy seat, between the two cherubims upon the ark of testimony.

8.text: The tabernacle candlestick was made of one piece of beaten gold, from its shaft to its flowers, after the pattern the Lord had shewed Moses.

17.title: The rod that blossomed
17.text: Aaron's rod was an almond branch; in Hebrew the word for almond evokes "watching/awaking," as the almond is the first tree to bloom in spring — a sign that God was awake to confirm the chosen priesthood.

36.title: The daughters who set a precedent
36.text: Zelophehad had no sons; in Numbers 27 his five daughters won the right to inherit their father's land. Numbers 36 closes the book by solving the next problem: how to keep that inheritance within the tribe of Manasseh.
